title: DIG: Oracle-Guided Directed Input Generation for One-Day Vulnerabilities
authors: Andrew Bao, Haochen Zeng, Peng Chen, Stephen McCamant, Pen-Chung Yew
abstract: One-day vulnerabilities pose significant risks due to delayed or incomplete patch adoption. Generating proof-of-concept (PoC) inputs is therefore essential for assessing real-world impact. The key challenge is identifying necessary constraints for triggering the vulnerability and solving them effectively. Existing directed fuzzing approaches prioritize inputs toward target locations, but neither explicitly identify necessary constraints nor solve them effectively, relying instead on target-distance feedback and random mutation. Agentic approaches show strong potential through code reasoning and structured input generation, but goal drift in long-horizon reasoning limits their effectiveness. DIG addresses this challenge by exploiting a key property of one-day vulnerabilities: patches often reveal necessary preconditions for triggering. DIG uses an LLM to analyze the patch and synthesize an oracle making these conditions explicit. The oracle supports effective PoC generation at two levels. At the high level, DIG performs oracle-guided generator evolution, where an agent infers and solves constraints to satisfy the oracle. At the low level, DIG instruments the oracle into the target program and uses branch-distance feedback to guide random mutation in directed fuzzing. Evaluation shows DIG outperforms 2 state-of-the-art agents and 10 fuzzers across 138 real-world CVEs. DIG triggers 80 vulnerabilities, surpassing prior results and outperforming the best baseline by 40% (57 vs. 80 CVEs). Notably, DIG exclusively triggers 9 vulnerabilities no existing technique can trigger. Compared to the average of other tools, DIG triggers vulnerabilities faster in 92.9% of cases, achieving over 100x speedup in 48.8% of cases, with a maximum speedup of 3,664x. Beyond one-day PoC generation, DIG uncovers 6 previously unknown vulnerabilities in widely deployed libraries, enabling zero-day discovery.
title_zh: DIG：Oracle引导的定向输入生成方法，用于已知漏洞（One-Day Vulnerabilities）
abstract_zh: 单日漏洞因补丁部署延迟或不完整而带来重大风险。因此，生成概念验证（PoC）输入对于评估实际影响至关重要。关键挑战在于识别触发漏洞所需的约束条件并有效求解。现有的定向模糊测试方法优先将输入导向目标位置，但既未明确识别必要约束条件，也未有效求解，而是依赖目标距离反馈和随机变异。智能体方法通过代码推理和结构化输入生成展现出强大潜力，但长程推理中的目标偏移限制了其有效性。DIG通过利用单日漏洞的一个关键属性来解决这一挑战：补丁通常揭示了触发漏洞的必要前提条件。DIG使用LLM分析补丁并合成一个预言，使这些条件显式化。该预言在两个层面支持高效的PoC生成。在高层面上，DIG执行预言引导的生成器进化，其中智能体推断并求解约束以满足预言。在低层面上，DIG将预言注入目标程序，并利用分支距离反馈指导定向模糊测试中的随机变异。评估表明，DIG在138个真实世界CVE中优于两个最先进的智能体和10个模糊测试工具。DIG触发了80个漏洞，超越了先前成果，比最佳基线高出40%（57个对比80个CVE）。值得注意的是，DIG独家触发了9个现有技术无法触发的漏洞。与其他工具的平均水平相比，DIG在92.9%的案例中更快触发漏洞，其中48.8%的案例实现了超过100倍的速度提升，最大加速比达3664倍。除单日PoC生成外，DIG还在广泛部署的库中发现了6个此前未知的漏洞，实现了零日漏洞发现。

title: Structured Testbench Generation for LLM-Driven HDL Design and Verification-Oriented Data Curation
authors: En-Ming Huang, Yu-Hung Kao, Ren-Hao Deng, Wei-Po Hsin, Yao-Ting Hsieh, Cheng Liang, Hsiang-Yu Tsou, Mu-Chi Chen, Yu-Kai Hung, Shao-Chun Ho, Po-Hsuang Huang, Shih-Hao Hung, H. T. Kung
abstract: Automated testbench generation has become a critical bottleneck in large language model (LLM)-driven Register Transfer Level (RTL) workflows, where large numbers of candidate designs must be verified rapidly and reliably. Existing prompt-based approaches treat testbench generation as unconstrained code synthesis, yielding stochastic outputs with high token cost, low reproducibility, and insufficient coverage. To address this gap, we present STG, a Structured Testbench Generation framework that exploits the inherent structure of hardware designs to generate deterministic testbenches. As a direct verification tool, STG runs 720x faster than an iterative LLM-based testbench generation flow and higher rate of successful compilation, achieves higher coverage, and reduces false-pass verdicts on incorrect DUTs. STG also helps identify errors in RTL generation benchmarks by exposing faulty benchmark testbenches. As a data curation engine, it is 11x faster than LLM-based filtering on a single CPU core with 127x less energy, and the resulting distilled models provide state-of-the-art performance in our multi-benchmark evaluation. As a test-time scaling oracle, it reduces node count by 14-47\%. Our models are available at https://huggingface.co/collections/AS-SiliconMind/siliconmind-v12.
title_zh: 面向LLM驱动的HDL设计与验证数据整理的结构化测试平台生成
abstract_zh: 在大型语言模型驱动的寄存器传输级工作流中，自动化测试台生成已成为关键瓶颈——大量候选设计必须快速且可靠地完成验证。现有的基于提示的方法将测试台生成视为无约束代码合成，产出随机性输出，存在令牌成本高、可重复性低且覆盖率不足的问题。为弥补这一空白，我们提出STG（结构化测试台生成框架），通过利用硬件设计的固有结构生成确定性测试台。作为直接验证工具，STG的运行速度比基于迭代式LLM的测试台生成流程快720倍，编译成功率更高，覆盖率更优，并能减少对错误被测设计的误判通过。此外，STG还能通过暴露有缺陷的基准测试台，帮助识别RTL生成基准中的错误。作为数据整理引擎，它在单CPU核心上的运行速度比基于LLM的过滤方法快11倍，能耗降低127倍，由此产生的蒸馏模型在多基准评估中实现了最先进的性能。作为测试时扩展预言，它将节点数减少了14%至47%。我们的模型可在https://huggingface.co/collections/AS-SiliconMind/siliconmind-v12获取。

title: Beyond Problem Solving: UOJ-Bench for Evaluating Code Generation, Hacking, and Repair in Competitive Programming
authors: Tingqiang Xu, Hangrui Zhou, Tianle Cai, Alex Gu, Kaifeng Lyu
abstract: Despite strong performance in competitive programming, the role of Large Language Models (LLMs) in supporting human learning in the same setting remains largely unexplored. In this work, we introduce UOJ-Bench, a benchmark designed to evaluate not only the problem-solving ability of LLMs, but also their ability to identify errors in human-written code -- a crucial educational activity traditionally supported by running test cases over online judge systems. UOJ-Bench consists of three distinct tasks: code generation, code hacking, and code repair, all constructed from real-world code submissions on the Universal Online Judge (UOJ) and evaluated through UOJ's native judging infrastructure. Our results show that under one-shot evaluation, even the strongest models fail to identify errors in more than 50% of a set of submissions that have been found to be incorrect by UOJ users. While test-time scaling improves success rates to above 90%, the substantial computational costs incurred from model inference limit its practicality for large-scale deployment. Despite these limitations, we find that the best-performing models under test-time scaling can uncover errors in over 5% of full-score submissions across roughly 30 problems, suggesting that frontier LLMs can already provide complementary signals beyond standard judging systems.
title_zh: 超越问题求解：UOJ-Bench用于评估竞赛编程中的代码生成、攻击与修复
abstract_zh: 尽管在编程竞赛中表现出色，但大语言模型在相同场景下支持人类学习的作用仍鲜有探索。本研究提出了UOJ-Bench基准测试，该基准不仅用于评估大语言模型的问题求解能力，还评估其识别人类编写代码中错误的能力——这种关键的教学生态活动传统上依赖在线评测系统的测试用例运行来完成。UOJ-Bench包含三项不同任务：代码生成、代码攻击和代码修复，所有任务均基于通用在线评测系统（UOJ）上的真实代码提交构建，并通过UOJ原生评测基础设施进行评估。结果表明，在一次性评估下，即使最强模型也无法识别超过50%的已被UOJ用户判定为错误的提交。尽管测试时扩展能将成功率提升至90%以上，但模型推理产生的高昂计算成本限制了其大规模部署的实用性。尽管存在这些局限，我们发现采用测试时扩展的最佳模型能在约30道题目中发现超过5%的满分提交中的错误，这表明前沿大语言模型已能提供超越标准评测系统的补充性信号。

title: Exploring Systems-Thinking Approaches to Loss of Control Risk
authors: Aurelio Carlucci, Sean P. Fillingham, James Walpole, Jakub Kryś
abstract: Internal deployment of agentic AI systems for coding and research creates a sociotechnical control problem that extends beyond model behaviour. We treat internal-deployment Loss of Control as the inability to reliably constrain, audit, reverse, or halt AI-mediated changes to code, infrastructure, evaluation, or deployment processes in time to prevent serious organisational or societal harms. We ask whether established systems-safety methods can identify risks that model-level evaluations may miss. Using a generic frontier-lab coding-agent scenario reconstructed from public materials, we apply STECA, STPA, and FRAM. The analyses surface complementary findings: published frameworks can leave governance responsibilities and feedback loops externally unverifiable; delays in monitoring and intervention can make otherwise valid control actions ineffective; and routine operational variability can gradually erode the calibration and independence of safeguards. We argue that frontier-AI risk management should pair model-focused evaluations with systems-level hazard analysis and operational assurance that tracks whether controls remain effective over time.
title_zh: 探索系统思维方法以应对失控风险
abstract_zh: 将代理型AI系统内部部署用于编码和研究，会引发一个超越模型行为本身的社会技术控制问题。我们将内部部署中的“失控”定义为：无法及时可靠地约束、审计、逆转或停止AI对代码、基础设施、评估或部署流程所做的更改，从而难以防范严重的组织或社会危害。我们探究既有的系统安全方法能否识别出模型层面评估可能遗漏的风险。基于公开资料重建一个通用前沿实验室的编码代理场景，我们应用了STECA、STPA和FRAM三种方法。分析结果揭示了互补性发现：现有框架可能导致治理责任和反馈回路在外部无法验证；监控与干预的延迟可能使原本有效的控制措施失效；而日常操作中的变异会逐渐侵蚀安全防护措施的校准精度和独立性。我们认为，前沿AI风险管理应将模型层面的评估与系统级危害分析及操作保障相结合，持续追踪控制措施是否长期有效。

title: Specifying Hardware Communication as Programs
authors: Ernest Ng, Nikil Shyamsunder, Francis Pham, Adrian Sampson, Kevin Laeufer
abstract: To test and debug hardware modules, it is common to write two programs: a driver, which translates high-level transactions into interactions on the module's input and output signals, and a monitor, which analyzes a signal-level execution trace and recognizes a transaction. These two programs are commonly implemented separately for each hardware protocol, but this separation entails manual effort and risks inconsistencies. We advocate an alternative approach. We present a DSL in which users specify hardware communication protocols as succinct imperative programs. Crucially, the same specification can be used to both drive designs and monitor transactions. We present the design of a tool, which given a specification in our DSL and a waveform, automatically infers a transaction-level trace consistent with the waveform. We discuss plans to evaluate our DSL on real-world interconnects such as Wishbone and AXI-Stream.
title_zh: 将硬件通信指定为程序
abstract_zh: 为了测试和调试硬件模块，通常需要编写两个程序：驱动程序（将高级事务转换为模块输入输出信号上的交互）和监视器（分析信号级执行轨迹并识别事务）。这两种程序通常针对每个硬件协议单独实现，但这种分离方式既需要人工操作，又存在不一致的风险。我们提出了一种替代方案：设计一种领域特定语言（DSL），用户在其中将硬件通信协议描述为简洁的命令式程序。关键在于，同一份规格说明既可用来驱动设计，也可用于监视事务。我们展示了一个工具的设计，该工具根据DSL规格和波形，自动推断出与波形一致的事务级轨迹。我们还讨论了在Wishbone和AXI-Stream等真实互连协议上评估该DSL的计划。

title: Recursive Agent Harnesses
authors: Elias Lumer, Sahil Sen, Kevin Paul, Vamse Kumar Subbiah
abstract: Recursive language models (RLMs) showed that recursion over model calls is an effective strategy for long-context reasoning, and production coding agents have begun to write code that spawns subagents at scale, most recently in Anthropic's dynamic workflows. We name and study the pattern between these two lines of work, where the recursive unit is a full agent harness with filesystem tools, code execution, and planning rather than a model call with no tools. We call this the Recursive Agent Harness (RAH) and frame it as harness recursion, the code-first extension to the model recursion of RLMs. A parent agent generates and runs an executable script that spawns subagent harnesses in parallel for fine-grained workloads and uses structured function calls for small subtasks. We provide a controlled evaluation on long-context reasoning. With the backbone held fixed at GPT-5 to match the published Codex and RLM baselines, RAH improves the Codex coding-agent baseline from 71.75% to 81.36% on Oolong-Synthetic (199 samples, 13 context-length buckets up to 4M tokens), a gain attributable to the harness rather than the model. With a stronger backbone, Claude Sonnet 4.5, the same design reaches 89.77%.
title_zh: 递归代理利用
abstract_zh: 递归语言模型（RLM）表明，在模型调用上进行递归是应对长上下文推理的有效策略，而生产级编码代理已开始编写可大规模生成子代理的代码——这一趋势近期在Anthropic的动态工作流中尤为明显。我们识别并研究了这两种工作之间的共性模式：其递归单元并非无工具辅助的模型调用，而是配备文件系统工具、代码执行能力和规划功能的完整代理框架。我们将此模式命名为递归代理框架（RAH），并将其定位为框架递归——即RLM模型递归的代码优先扩展。父代理生成并执行可执行脚本，该脚本并行生成子代理框架以处理细粒度工作负载，同时通过结构化函数调用处理小型子任务。我们提供了长上下文推理的受控评估。在保持骨干模型固定为GPT-5以匹配已发表的Codex和RLM基线的前提下，RAH将Codex编码代理基线在Oolong-Synthetic数据集（199个样本，13个上下文长度区间，最长400万token）上的性能从71.75%提升至81.36%——这一提升归因于框架本身而非模型。当采用更强的骨干模型Claude Sonnet 4.5时，相同设计可达到89.77%。

title: AgentRivet: an automated system for producing Rivet routines from journal publications
authors: Antonio J. Costa, Caterina Doglioni, Christian Gütschow, Andrew D. Pilkington, Sukanya Sinha
abstract: Particle physics collider experiments provide Rivet routines as part of the analysis preservation strategy for model-independent measurements. Rivet is a C++ toolkit that allow new theoretical models to be compared to the measurements, thus aiding the development and tuning of Monte Carlo event generators as well as searches for physics beyond the Standard Model. However, analysis coverage is known to be incomplete, with only 39% of measurements having documented and publicly available Rivet routines. In this article, we design and implement an automated workflow based on Large Language Models with the goal of providing the missing routines. This multi-step workflow, referred to as AgentRivet, extracts the physics analysis information from published papers and writes the missing Rivet routines, with intermediate code- and physics- reviews as part of an autonomous quality control. We report the results obtained using commercial Large Language Models, provided by OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google, for two recent measurements from the ATLAS and CMS experiments. We find that AgentRivet produces competent Rivet routines with few syntax errors. The physics fidelity of the routines is reasonable and follows the explanations given in the relevant publications. Nevertheless, physics-implementation issues do arise and are investigated using the artefacts produced by AgentRivet. The majority of physics implementation issues arise from subtle-but-ambiguous definitions in the given publication, although some models struggle to implement complex observables even when clear definitions are given.
title_zh: AgentRivet：一个从期刊出版物自动生成Rivet例程的系统
abstract_zh: 粒子物理对撞机实验提供Rivet例程作为模型无关测量的分析保存策略的一部分。Rivet是一个C++工具包，允许将新的理论模型与测量结果进行比较，从而帮助开发和调优蒙特卡洛事件生成器，并寻找超越标准模型的新物理。然而，已知分析覆盖率并不完整，仅有39%的测量结果具有公开且可获取的Rivet例程文档。本文基于大语言模型设计并实现了一个自动化工作流程，旨在补充缺失的例程。这一多步骤工作流程（称为AgentRivet）从已发表的论文中提取物理分析信息，并编写缺失的Rivet例程，同时将中间代码审查和物理审查作为自主质量控制的一部分。我们报告了使用OpenAI、Anthropic和Google提供的商业大语言模型对ATLAS和CMS实验的两项最新测量结果所获得的结果。我们发现AgentRivet能够生成语法错误很少的合格Rivet例程。这些例程的物理保真度合理，且遵循相关出版物中的解释。尽管如此，物理实现问题确实会出现，并通过AgentRivet产生的制品进行了研究。大多数物理实现问题源于给定出版物中微妙且模糊的定义，但某些模型即使在给出清晰定义的情况下，也难以实现复杂的观测量。

title: Extended Frege proofs, circuits and rewriting
authors: Jan Krajicek
abstract: Inspired by a statement about Extended Frege proof systems by Jain and Jin (FOCS 2022) we prove that: - there is a p-time binary relation $\approx$ between circuits that implies their logical equivalence, - the relation $\approx$ implies that each of the two circuits can be rewritten into the other one by possibly deleting some gates and adding at most seven new gates, - if the equivalence $C \equiv D$ has a size $s$ proof in an Extended Frege or a Circuit Frege proof system then there is a chain of circuits $E_i$ $$ C = E_0 \approx \dots \approx E_t = D $$ with $t \le s^{O(1)}$.
title_zh: 扩展弗雷格证明、电路与重写
abstract_zh:
受 Jain 和 Jin 在 FOCS 2022 上关于扩展弗雷格证明系统的一个陈述的启发，我们证明：
- 在电路之间存在一个多项式时间二元关系 $\approx$，它蕴含了它们的逻辑等价性；
- 关系 $\approx$ 蕴含了这两个电路中的每一个都可以通过可能删除一些门并添加最多七个新门来改写成另一个；
- 如果等价性 $C \equiv D$ 在扩展弗雷格或电路弗雷格证明系统中有一个大小为 $s$ 的证明，那么存在一个电路链 $E_i$
  $$ C = E_0 \approx \dots \approx E_t = D $$
  且 $t \le s^{O(1)}$。

title: Functional Cache Grafting: Robust and Rapid Code-Policy Synthesis for Embodied Agents
authors: Saehun Chun, Wonje Choi, Sera Choi, Sanghyun Ahn, Honguk Woo
abstract: Code-writing large language models (CodeLLMs) generate executable code policies for embodied agents by translating natural language goals and environmental constraints into structured control programs. However, policy generation in open-domain embodied environments suffers from two fundamental limitations: (i) delayed decoding caused by repetitive prefill computation over long prompts, and (ii) limited robustness due to fully generative decoding, which often produces API mismatches, missing safety guards, and unstable control logic. To address these limitations, we present FCGraft, a Functional Cache Grafting framework. FCGraft maintains a library of function-level validated code skeletons and their associated prompt-level Transformer key-value (KV) caches, and synthesizes new policies by retrieving relevant functions and grafting their KV caches when a new task is provided. Given retrieved function caches, FCGraft performs cache grafting via stitching, which composes cached function segments into a composite policy, and patching, which locally adapts only the necessary code regions to satisfy task-specific parameters and constraints with minimal additional decoding. By eliminating redundant prefill computation, this approach reduces generation latency, while reusing validated control structures improves robustness over prompt-level caching methods RAGCache, achieving 18.31% higher task success rate and 2.3x faster policy synthesis.
title_zh: 功能缓存嫁接：面向具身智能体的鲁棒且快速的代码策略合成
abstract_zh: 代码编写大型语言模型（CodeLLMs）通过将自然语言目标和环境约束转化为结构化控制程序，为具身智能体生成可执行的代码策略。然而，在开放域具身环境中，策略生成存在两个根本性限制：（i）由于长提示的重复预填充计算导致的延迟解码，以及（ii）完全生成式解码导致的鲁棒性不足，往往产生API不匹配、缺失安全防护以及不稳定的控制逻辑。为了解决这些限制，我们提出了FCGraft——一种功能缓存嫁接框架。FCGraft维护一个函数级验证代码骨架及其关联的提示级Transformer键值（KV）缓存库，并在新任务出现时通过检索相关函数并嫁接其KV缓存来合成新策略。给定检索到的函数缓存后，FCGraft通过拼接（stitching）和修补（patching）进行缓存嫁接：拼接将缓存的函数片段组合成复合策略，修补则仅局部调整必要的代码区域以满足任务特定参数和约束，从而最小化额外解码。通过消除冗余的预填充计算，该方法降低了生成延迟，而重用经过验证的控制结构使得鲁棒性优于提示级缓存方法RAGCache，任务成功率提高18.31%，策略合成速度提升2.3倍。

title: Multi-Objective Coevolution of Prompts and Templates for Circuit Approximation
authors: Martin Tomasovic, Lukas Sekanina
abstract: Approximate multipliers deliberately relax computational accuracy to achieve gains in power efficiency, latency, and silicon area, which makes them well-suited for error-resilient applications such as neural networks. In this work, we introduce a co-evolutionary algorithm that leverages an off-the-shelf large language model (LLM) without requiring domain-specific training to automate the design of optimized 8-bit approximate multipliers. The approach simultaneously evolves a population of candidate circuits and a population of prompt templates that steer LLM-driven modifications. Experimental results for several target design objectives demonstrate that the proposed method discovers approximate multipliers with improved error-area trade-offs compared to highly optimized circuits from the EvoApproxLib library.
title_zh: 用于电路近似的提示与模板多目标协同进化
abstract_zh: 近似乘法器有意放宽计算精度，以换取功耗效率、延迟和硅面积方面的优化，这使得它们非常适用于误差容忍型应用，如神经网络。在本研究中，我们提出了一种协同进化算法，该算法利用现成的大型语言模型（LLM），无需特定领域的训练即可自动设计优化的8位近似乘法器。该方法同时进化候选电路群体和引导LLM驱动修改的提示模板群体。针对多个目标设计目标的实验结果表明，与EvoApproxLib库中高度优化的电路相比，所提出的方法能够发现具有更好误差-面积权衡的近似乘法器。

title: SoK: The Constant Time Model
authors: Billy Bob Brumley
abstract: Constant time programming patterns is the primary defense against timing attacks on cryptographic implementations, yet what "constant time" means varies across academia and industry. This work systematizes constant time models and their evolution, identifies a recurring gap between what models protect and what specifications assume, and distills an offensive methodology for discovering timing vulnerabilities that originate outside the cryptographic primitive boundary. Applying this methodology, we locate a specification-level vulnerability related to private key loading, and confirm the leak in both OpenSSL and BoringSSL. Counterintuitively, BoringSSL's per-observation signal is several orders of magnitude stronger than OpenSSL's, despite an explicitly stricter threat model.
title_zh: SoK：恒定时间模型
abstract_zh: 常量时间编程模式是防范密码实现中时间攻击的首要手段，然而“常量时间”的定义在学术界和工业界并不统一。本研究系统梳理了常量时间模型及其演进过程，指出了模型保护范围与规范假设之间反复出现的差异，并提炼出一种攻击性方法论，用于发现源于密码原语边界之外的时间漏洞。应用该方法论，我们定位了一个与私钥加载相关的规范级漏洞，并在OpenSSL和BoringSSL中确认了该信息泄露。与直觉相反的是，尽管BoringSSL的安全威胁模型更加严格，其每次观测的信号强度却比OpenSSL高出数个数量级。

title: SpatialClaw: Rethinking Action Interface for Agentic Spatial Reasoning
authors: Seokju Cho, Ryo Hachiuma, Abhishek Badki, Hang Su, Byung-Kwan Lee, Chan Hee Song, Sifei Liu, Subhashree Radhakrishnan, Seungryong Kim, Yu-Chiang Frank Wang, Min-Hung Chen
abstract: Spatial reasoning, the ability to determine where objects are, how they relate, and how they move in 3D, remains a fundamental challenge for vision-language models (VLMs). Tool-augmented agents attempt to address this by augmenting VLMs with specialist perception modules, yet their effectiveness is bounded by the action interface through which those tools are invoked. In this work, we study how the design of this interface shapes the agent's capacity for open-ended spatial reasoning. Existing spatial agents either employ single-pass code execution, which commits to a full analysis strategy before any intermediate result is observed, or rely on a structured tool-call interface that often offers less flexibility for freely composing operations or tailoring the analysis to each task. Both designs offer limited flexibility for open-ended, complex 3D/4D spatial reasoning. We therefore propose SpatialClaw, a training-free framework for spatial reasoning that adopts code as the action interface. SpatialClaw maintains a stateful Python kernel pre-loaded with input frames and a suite of perception and geometry primitives, letting a VLM-backed agent write one executable cell per step conditioned on all prior outputs, enabling the agent to flexibly compose and manipulate perception results and adapt its analysis to both intermediate text and visual observations and the demands of each problem. Evaluated across 20 spatial reasoning benchmarks spanning a broad range of static and dynamic 3D/4D spatial reasoning tasks, SpatialClaw achieves 59.9% average accuracy, outperforming the recent spatial agent by +11.2 points, with consistent gains across six VLM backbones from two model families without any benchmark- or model-specific adaptation.
title_zh: SpatialClaw：重新思考智能体空间推理的动作接口
abstract_zh: 空间推理——即判断物体位置、相互关系及三维运动方式的能力——仍然是视觉语言模型（VLM）面临的基础性挑战。工具增强代理试图通过为VLM添加专业感知模块来解决这一问题，但其效果受限于调用这些工具所依赖的动作接口。在本研究中，我们探讨了该接口的设计如何塑造代理在开放式空间推理中的能力。现有的空间代理要么采用单次代码执行（在观察到任何中间结果之前就确定完整的分析策略），要么依赖结构化的工具调用接口（该接口在灵活组合操作或为每个任务定制分析方面往往不够灵活）。这两种设计都限制了开放式、复杂的3D/4D空间推理的灵活性。为此，我们提出了SpatialClaw——一种基于代码作为动作接口的无训练空间推理框架。SpatialClaw维护了一个有状态的Python内核，该内核预先加载了输入帧以及一套感知和几何基元，使基于VLM的代理能够根据所有先前的输出逐步编写可执行的单元格，从而灵活组合和操控感知结果，并根据中间文本与视觉观测以及每个问题的需求调整分析。在涵盖广泛静态与动态3D/4D空间推理任务的20个空间推理基准测试中，SpatialClaw实现了59.9%的平均准确率，比最新的空间代理高出11.2个百分点，并且在来自两个模型家族的六种VLM骨干网络上均表现出一致的性能提升，无需针对基准或模型进行任何特定适配。

title: The End of Code Review: Coding Agents Supersede Human Inspection
authors: Martin Monperrus
abstract: Code review has been the primary quality gate in software development since Fagan formalised code inspection in 1976. For five decades, having a human examine and comment on a colleague's changes before merge has been a cornerstone practice at organisations of every size. Coding agents are large language model (LLM)-based autonomous systems capable of reading, writing, testing, and repairing software. We argue that coding agents have crossed a threshold of capability at which traditional human code review is no longer a necessary component of a software quality pipeline. Our argument rests on two claims: every stated goal of code review can be served by agents at lower cost and higher throughput; the naive integration in which agents write code and humans remain the mandatory reviewers is a dead end because it neither provides meaningful assurance nor scales with AI-assisted throughput.
title_zh: 代码审查的终结：编码代理取代人工审查
abstract_zh: 自1976年法根（Fagan）将代码审查正式化以来，代码审查一直是软件开发中的主要质量把关措施。五十年来，在合并前由人工审查并评价同事的代码变更，已成为各规模组织中的一项基石惯例。编程代理是基于大语言模型（LLM）的自主系统，能够阅读、编写、测试和修复软件。我们认为，编程代理已跨越一个能力阈值——传统人工代码审查已不再是软件质量流水线中的必要环节。这一观点基于两项主张：代码审查的每一个既定目标都能由代理以更低成本、更高吞吐量实现；那种由代理编写代码、人工强制审查的简单整合方式是一条死路——因为它既无法提供有意义的保障，也无法随AI辅助的吞吐量进行扩展。

title: Getting Better at Working With You: Compiling User Corrections into Runtime Enforcement for Coding Agents
authors: Yujun Zhou, Kehan Guo, Haomin Zhuang, Xiangqi Wang, Yue Huang, Zhenwen Liang, Pin-Yu Chen, Tian Gao, Nuno Moniz, Nitesh V. Chawla, Xiangliang Zhang
abstract: Interactive LLM agents are becoming part of daily work, but they do not reliably become easier to work with over time: a correction remembered in one session may still be violated in the next. We study this gap between preference access and preference compliance. In tasks derived from anonymized real-user friction cases, Mem0 memory still leaves 57.5% of applicable preference checks violated. We introduce Test-time Rule Acquisition and Compiled Enforcement (TRACE), a drop-in skill-layer pipeline for coding-agent runtimes that mines user corrections, rewrites them as atomic rules, and compiles them into runtime checks that must pass before an agent completes future tasks. Unlike runtime checks written ahead of time by developers, TRACE skills come from the user's own chat corrections. We evaluate TRACE with simulated user-in-the-loop experiments on ClawArena coding-agent tasks and MemoryArena-derived memory-intensive tasks. On ClawArena, TRACE reduces held-out preference violation from 100.0% to 37.6% on in-distribution tasks and from 100.0% to 2.0% on out-of-distribution tasks. On MemoryArena-derived tasks, TRACE reduces in-distribution violation from 100.0% to 60.5% while matching or exceeding the strongest memory baseline on task pass. These results suggest that compiling corrections into runtime enforcement can address a repeated-friction failure mode that memory alone does not reliably solve, reducing the need for users to restate the same correction across future sessions. Experiment code is available at https://github.com/YujunZhou/TRACE_exp, and the deployable skill is available at https://github.com/YujunZhou/tellonce.
title_zh: 更好地与你合作：将用户修正编译为编码代理的运行时强制执行
abstract_zh: 交互式大语言模型（LLM）代理正逐步融入日常工作，但它们并不会随着时间推移而变得更加可靠：在一个会话中被记住的修正指令，下一个会话中仍可能被违反。我们研究了偏好获取与偏好遵从之间的这种差距。在基于匿名真实用户摩擦案例的任务中，Mem0 记忆仍然导致 57.5% 的适用偏好检查被违反。我们提出了测试时规则获取与编译执行（TRACE）方法，这是一个即插即用的技能层管线，适用于编码代理运行时环境。该方法可挖掘用户修正指令，将其重写为原子规则，并编译成运行时检查项——这些检查项必须在代理完成未来任务之前通过。不同于由开发者提前编写的运行时检查，TRACE 技能来源于用户自己的聊天修正。我们使用模拟用户参与实验，在 ClawArena 编码代理任务和 MemoryArena 衍生的内存密集型任务上评估了 TRACE。在 ClawArena 中，TRACE 将分布内任务上的未见过偏好违反率从 100.0% 降至 37.6%，将分布外任务上的违反率从 100.0% 降至 2.0%。在 MemoryArena 衍生任务中，TRACE 将分布内违反率从 100.0% 降至 60.5%，同时在任务通过率上匹配或超越了最强的记忆基线。这些结果表明，将修正指令编译为运行时强制执行，可以解决纯记忆无法可靠解决的重复摩擦故障模式，从而减少用户在未来会话中重复相同修正的需求。实验代码见 https://github.com/YujunZhou/TRACE_exp，可部署技能见 https://github.com/YujunZhou/tellonce。

title: Toward Instructions-as-Code: Understanding the Impact of Instruction Files on Agentic Pull Requests
authors: Ali Arabat, Mohammed Sayagh
abstract: AI-agents (e.g., GitHub Copilot) collaborate as teammates in different software engineering tasks, including code generation proposed through pull requests (Agentic-PRs). For better agent efficiency, developers create instruction files that guide the AI-agents, including how to navigate the project, locate the right components, run tests, respect best practices, and more. In this paper, we investigate the relationship between the creation of these instructions and the performance of AI-agents in creating better pull requests, which have a higher chance of success (i.e., the merge rate), address more complex tasks (e.g., code churn), and require less effort to be merged (e.g., time to merge). To this end, we analyze 15,549 agentic PRs from 148 projects in the AIDev dataset. Using the three dimensions, we compare each project before and after the creation of the instruction files. We find that specifying instructions for AI-agents does not necessarily lead to better results. With the instruction files, 27.7\% of the projects increased their merge rate by at least 20\%, while 26.35\% decreased it. The same observation is seen with the amount of changes (e.g., code churn, number of modified files) and with the efforts to merge an agentic PR (e.g., merge time and number of comments). From a first exploration, we find that projects that managed to increase their merge rate have substantially longer instruction files, which are also well structured into a higher number of sections and sub-sections. Our results motivate the need for research to assist practitioners in framing the development of instruction files as a software engineering activity (aka, \textbf{Instructions-as-Code}).
title_zh: 迈向指令即代码：理解指令文件对智能体拉取请求的影响
abstract_zh: AI代理（如GitHub Copilot）在多项软件工程任务中作为团队成员进行协作，包括通过拉取请求生成代码（即Agentic-PRs）。为提升代理效率，开发者会创建指导文件，引导AI代理如何浏览项目、定位正确组件、运行测试、遵循最佳实践等。本文探讨了这些指导文件的创建与AI代理表现之间的关系，具体关注其能否生成更优的拉取请求（如更高的合并率）、处理更复杂的任务（如代码变更量），以及降低合并所需的工作量（如合并时间）。为此，我们分析了AIDev数据集中148个项目的15,549个Agentic-PRs。从上述三个维度出发，比较各项目在创建指导文件前后的表现。结果表明，为AI代理指定指导文件并不必然带来更好的结果。引入指导文件后，27.7%的项目合并率提升至少20%，但同时也有26.35%的项目合并率下降。类似的趋势也体现在变更量（如代码变更量、修改文件数）和合并Agentic-PR所需的工作量（如合并时间、评论数量）上。初步探索发现，合并率提升的项目其指导文件明显更长，且结构更完善，包含更多章节和子章节。本研究结果揭示了将指导文件的制定视为一项软件工程活动（即“指令即代码”）的研究必要性，以帮助实践者优化这一流程。

title: Structuring Transparency: Developing Domain-Specific Generative AI Declaration Frameworks in Higher Education
authors: Nicholas Micallef, Olga Petrovska
abstract: As Generative AI (GenAI) disrupts higher education, institutions increasingly require students to declare AI use. However, generic, binary declarations (e.g., "I used GenAI") fail to capture the nuanced application of these tools in different academic tasks. Establishing transparency is key to protecting academic integrity, promoting AI literacy, and shifting the focus from policing to professional practice. In response, this paper contributes a design artefact and an accompanying position: a framework of two task-specific declaration structures, one for writing-focused activities and one for coding assessments, developed for a Computer Science department on the basis of an existing taxonomy of GenAI usage, together with an argument that task-specific disclosure is needed to move beyond binary declarations. By categorising AI usage across specific cognitive and developmental stages, such as structural planning vs. Textual Content Generation, or code improvement vs. code generation, the framework encourages students to reflect on their own learning process and clarifies the boundary between acceptable assistance and academic misconduct. We propose this domain-specific approach as a foundation for fostering more honest assessment in Computer Science and other disciplines, aiming to better prepare students for professional environments where documenting GenAI workflows might be an essential job requirement.
title_zh: 构建透明度：在高等教育中开发领域特定的生成式人工智能声明框架
abstract_zh: 随着生成式人工智能（GenAI）对高等教育的颠覆性影响，院校日益要求学生声明其使用AI的情况。然而，笼统的二元声明（例如“我使用了GenAI”）无法捕捉这些工具在不同学术任务中的细微应用。建立透明度对于维护学术诚信、提升AI素养以及将关注点从监管转向专业实践至关重要。为此，本文贡献了一项设计产物及相应立场：基于现有GenAI使用分类法，为计算机科学系开发了一个包含两种任务特定声明结构的框架——一种针对写作类活动，另一种针对编程评估——同时论证需要超越二元声明，采用任务特定的披露方式。通过将AI使用归类至特定的认知与发展阶段（如结构规划 vs. 文本内容生成，或代码改进 vs. 代码生成），该框架鼓励学生反思自身学习过程，并明确可接受帮助与学术不端之间的界限。我们提出这一领域特异性方法，旨在为计算机科学及其他学科更诚实的评估奠定基础，帮助学生更好地适应未来职场环境——在那里，记录GenAI工作流程可能成为一项基本职业要求。

title: MDForge: Agentic Molecular Dynamics Pipeline Design under Sparse Simulator Feedback
authors: Zehong Wang, Yijun Ma, Connor R. Schmidt, Tianyi Ma, Weixiang Sun, Ziming Li, Xiaoguang Guo, Chuxu Zhang, Matthew J. Webber, Yanfang Ye
abstract: Molecular dynamics (MD) is the canonical in-silico method for atomistic molecular science, simulating molecular behavior from first-principle physics. Designing an MD pipeline for a new system requires substantial expert knowledge: running it on even one molecule is expensive, ruling out trial-and-error. We automate this expert pipeline-design process with an LLM agent. Unlike existing MD agents that orchestrate a predefined tool set, we treat pipeline design as open-ended code generation in which the agent's behavior is reshaped online by verbal reward. Specifically, we build MDForge, an LLM agent whose in-context update rule densifies the sparse reward via a multi-agent debate among physics experts. On three SAMPL host-guest binding free-energy benchmarks, MDForge automatically designs MD pipelines competitive with human experts. Deployed on a library of unseen candidate guests, its CB[7] pipeline discovers a novel binder that wet-lab competition NMR confirms is a high-affinity, picomolar CB[7] binder. Our data and code are available at https://github.com/Zehong-Wang/MDForge.
title_zh: MDForge：稀疏模拟器反馈下的智能分子动力学流程设计
abstract_zh: 分子动力学（MD）是基于第一性原理物理学模拟分子行为的原子级分子科学经典计算机模拟方法。为新型体系设计MD管线需要大量专业知识：即便仅针对单个分子运行MD模拟成本也极高，这排除了试错法的可行性。我们利用大语言模型（LLM）代理实现了这一专家级管线设计流程的自动化。与现有通过编排预定义工具集运行的MD代理不同，我们将管线设计视为开放式代码生成问题，代理的行为通过口头奖励在线重塑。具体而言，我们构建了MDForge——其上下文更新规则通过物理专家间的多智能体辩论使稀疏奖励变得密集。在三个SAMPL主客体结合自由能基准测试中，MDForge自动设计的MD管线达到了与人类专家相媲美的性能。将其部署在未见候选客体的化合物库后，其设计的CB[7]空腔管线发现了一种新型结合剂，经湿实验室竞争核磁共振（NMR）证实为高亲和力、皮摩尔级的CB[7]结合剂。我们的数据和代码已公开于 https://github.com/Zehong-Wang/MDForge。

title: Understanding the Rejection of Fixes Generated by Agentic Pull Requests -- Insights from the AIDev Dataset
authors: Mahmoud Abujadallah, Ali Arabat, Mohammed Sayagh
abstract: AI coding agents are increasingly used to generate pull requests (PRs) that propose code fixes in software projects. From a first exploration of the AIDev dataset, we find that 46.41\% of the fixes proposed by the agents Copilot, Devin, Cursor, and Claude are rejected. This represents a significant amount of wasted resources that require human reviews, verifications, and running tests and validations for fixes that are merely discarded. Our goal in this paper is to understand the failure modes of AI-agents, an understanding that is crucial for better integrating AI-agents as efficient teammates. In this paper, we conduct a qualitative study on a representative sample of 306 non-merged pull requests created or co-authored by the agents mentioned earlier, followed by a quantitative analysis of the reasons for rejection. Our qualitative findings identify 14 reasons divided into four high-level categories for rejecting AI-agent fixes. We observe that developers can reject fixes due to fixes whose implementation is incorrect (e.g., incomplete, wrong approach), fixes that do not pass the continuous integration (CI) pipelines and fail tests, fixes for which the agent is unable to perform the implementation (e.g., no code generated, sessions lost), and fixes whose priority is low. Our results shed light on the importance of better guiding the model at these levels: (1) proposing hints about the approach to follow for fixing an issue, (2) outlining constraints or limitations regarding the approaches that should not be taken, and (3) instructing the agent on how to validate the implementation through CI pipelines and without introducing a breaking change. Our results suggest the need for good prioritization of tasks so that generated fixes do not lead to wasted human review efforts or wasted agent resources (e.g., tokens, compute, or allowed number of requests).
title_zh: 理解智能体生成的拉取请求中修复被拒绝的现象——来自AIDev数据集的洞见
abstract_zh: AI编程代理正被越来越多地用于生成拉取请求，以在软件项目中提出代码修复方案。通过对AIDev数据集的初步探索，我们发现Copilot、Devin、Cursor和Claude等代理提出的修复方案中有46.41%被拒绝。这意味着大量资源被浪费，包括人工审查、验证、测试及运行的投入，而最终这些修复方案却被弃用。本文旨在理解AI代理的失败模式，这一理解对于更好地将AI代理集成为高效协作伙伴至关重要。我们选取了由上述代理创建或合著的306个未合并拉取请求作为代表性样本，先开展定性研究，随后对拒绝原因进行定量分析。定性研究结果归纳出14个拒绝原因，可分为四大类：因实现方式不当（如不完整、错误方法）导致的修复被拒；因未通过持续集成管道或测试失败的修复被拒；因代理无法完成实现（如未生成代码、会话丢失）导致的修复被拒；以及因修复优先级较低被拒。我们的研究揭示了在以下层面优化模型引导的重要性：（1）提出修复问题的建议方法；（2）明确不应采用的方法的约束或限制；（3）指导代理如何通过持续集成管道验证实现，同时不引入破坏性变更。研究结果表明，需对任务进行合理优先级排序，以避免生成的修复方案造成人工审查资源或代理资源（如令牌、计算资源或允许请求次数）的浪费。

title: EconCSLib: AI-Assisted Lean Formalization for Economics & Computation research
authors: Nikhil Garg
abstract: This paper presents EconCSLib, a Lean 4 library and workflow for formalizing research papers in Economics and Computation with language-model assistance. The central design principle is a human-AI-Lean workflow: an LLM writes Lean code, Lean checks formal statements and proofs, and humans (assisted by an LLM) verify the translation boundary from paper claims to formal statements. EconCSLib is organized around research papers, preserving their formal statements and following their proof structure to the extent possible; reusable mathematical statements are elevated into shared EconCS infrastructure. The workflow is designed to be author-facing: researchers can formalize their own papers, inspect the Lean code's translations of paper-facing statements, and contribute reusable components back to the library; this is supported by post-formalization validation reports, paper result dependency graphs, and a review dashboard. The current public repository contains 11 formalized papers and 3 partially formalized papers, along with initial libraries for probability, auctions, matching markets, and graph tools. The library and workflow are available at https://github.com/nikhgarg/EconCSLib, with corresponding project webpage at https://gargnikhil.com/EconCSLib/. To our knowledge, we are also among the first applied math researchers to systematically pursue Lean formalization of one's own publications in the process of building such a community library. We welcome users and contributors to the project.
title_zh: EconCSLib：面向经济学与计算研究的AI辅助精益形式化
abstract_zh: 本文介绍了EconCSLib，这是一个Lean 4库及其工作流程，用于在语言模型辅助下形式化经济学与计算领域的科研论文。其核心设计原则是人-人工智能-Lean工作流：大语言模型编写Lean代码，Lean检查形式化陈述和证明，而人类（在大语言模型辅助下）验证从论文主张到形式化陈述的翻译边界。EconCSLib围绕科研论文组织，尽可能保留其形式化陈述并遵循其证明结构；可复用的数学表述被提升为共享的EconCS基础设施。该工作流程面向作者设计：研究人员可以形式化自己的论文，检查Lean代码对论文陈述的翻译，并将可复用的组件贡献回库中；这由形式化后验证报告、论文结果依赖图和审阅面板支持。当前公共代码库包含11篇已形式化论文和3篇部分形式化论文，以及概率、拍卖、匹配市场和图工具的初始库。该库及工作流程可从https://github.com/nikhgarg/EconCSLib 获取，对应项目页面为https://gargnikhil.com/EconCSLib/。据我们所知，我们也是首批在构建此类社区库过程中系统性地使用Lean对自己论文进行形式化的应用数学研究者之一。我们欢迎用户和贡献者参与该项目。

title: Mining Architectural Quality Under Agentic AI Adoption: A Causal Study of Java Repositories
authors: Oliver Aleksander Larsen, Mahyar T. Moghaddam
abstract: AI coding tools are now used by a majority of developers, and agentic use of these tools has popularized the practice colloquially called "vibe coding". Yet causal evidence on their effect on software architecture is scarce. Prior causal work has measured code-level outcomes (complexity, static analysis warnings); whether such degradation propagates to architecture-level outcomes remains unknown. We mine 151 open-source Java repositories, 74 with detectable agentic AI adoption (identified via configuration files and Co-Authored-By commit trailers) and 77 propensity-matched controls, across a 13-month per-repository window yielding 1,811 monthly Arcan snapshots. We estimate the causal effect of adoption on architectural smell density (ASD) with a staggered difference-in-differences design and the Borusyak imputation estimator, applying a causal design recently used for code-level metrics to the architecture level. Total smell counts are essentially unchanged (+1.1%, p = 0.82) while lines of code grow +12.8% (p = 0.003); the resulting 6.7% ASD decline (p = 0.004) is therefore a denominator effect rather than an architectural improvement. Per-type estimates and robustness checks (wild cluster bootstrap, Lee bounds, stale-observation sensitivity) corroborate the pattern; pre-trends are flat (Wald p = 0.90), consistent with parallel trends. Density-normalized outcomes can mislead when treatment affects system size: raw counts and explicit decomposition are required for causal mining studies of AI tool adoption. The complete replication package, including the curated 151-repository monthly panel, is publicly available.
title_zh: 代理式AI采用下的架构质量挖掘：Java仓库的因果研究
abstract_zh: 目前，大多数开发者正在使用AI编码工具，这些工具的“智能体化”应用催生了一种俗称“氛围编码”的实践方式。然而，关于这些工具对软件架构影响的因果证据仍然匮乏。以往的因果研究主要衡量代码层面的结果（如复杂度、静态分析警告），尚不清楚这种退化是否会蔓延至架构层面。我们挖掘了151个开源Java仓库，其中74个被检测出采用了智能体化AI（通过配置文件和“Co-Authored-By”提交尾注识别），并与77个通过倾向得分匹配的对照仓库进行对比，在每仓库13个月的时间窗口内收集了1,811份月度Arcan快照。我们采用交错双重差分设计和Borusyak归因估计法，将此前用于代码级指标的因果设计应用于架构层面，评估采用AI对架构异味密度（ASD）的因果效应。不雅的总体数量基本不变（+1.1%，p = 0.82），而代码行数增长了12.8%（p = 0.003）；由此导致的6.7%的ASD下降（p = 0.004）实际上是一个分母效应，而非架构改进。按类型估计和稳健性检验（野生集群自助法、Lee边界、陈旧观测敏感性）均证实了这一模式；事前趋势平缓（Wald p = 0.90），符合平行趋势假设。当处理效应影响系统规模时，密度标准化结果可能产生误导：因果挖掘研究需要原始计数和明确分解才能正确分析AI工具采纳的影响。完整的复现包（包括经过整理的151个仓库月度面板数据）已公开提供。

title: Detecting Functional Memorization in Code Language Models
authors: Matthieu Meeus, Anil Ramakrishna, Matthew Grange, Zheng Xu, Luca Melis
abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used to generate code at scale. Meanwhile, prior work has investigated whether training data may be recoverable from model outputs, by auditing the textual overlap between training examples and model generations. Code, however, can be functionally equivalent while textually dissimilar. In this work, we study functional memorization: extraction of functional logic beyond what verbatim metrics detect. We construct a counterfactual setup for Olmo-3-32B, comparing a midtrained model (exposed to target code) against a pretrained reference (not exposed). We prompt both models with Python function signatures and measure both textual and functional similarity (i.e., LLM-as-a-judge, execution-based). Our results show clear evidence of functional memorization, highlighting the need for auditing metrics that go beyond textual overlap.
title_zh: 检测代码语言模型中的功能记忆
abstract_zh: 大型语言模型（LLMs）正越来越多地被用于大规模生成代码。与此同时，先前的研究通过审计训练示例与模型生成内容之间的文本重叠，探讨了训练数据是否可能从模型输出中恢复。然而，代码可能在功能上等价，但在文本上却不相似。在本研究中，我们研究了功能记忆化：提取超越逐字指标所能检测到的功能逻辑。我们为 Olmo-3-32B 模型构建了一个反事实设置，将中期训练模型（暴露于目标代码）与预训练参考模型（未暴露于目标代码）进行对比。我们向这两个模型输入 Python 函数签名，并测量文本相似性和功能相似性（即，以大语言模型作为评判标准、基于执行的方法）。我们的结果明确显示了功能记忆化的证据，凸显了需要超越文本重叠的审计指标。

title: Estimating Technical Debt with Machine Learning: Leveraging Code Complexity and Developer Interactions
authors: Md Abu Sufian, Estiyak Rubaiat, Md Daud Hossain Supto, Nabajit Dey, Tajmun Nahar Tisha, Shafiqul Islam Nayem
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: Estimating technical debt (TD) is crucial in managing large-scale software projects. This study introduces a machine learning-based framework for TD estimation in opensource Java projects, integrating code complexity metrics and developer interaction features. Using a dataset of 55 million Java files from 100,000 GitHub repositories, we apply XGBoost, LSTM, and Informer models for multi-horizon TD forecasting. SHAP (SHapley Additive Explanations) is utilized for interpretability, revealing key TD drivers such as cyclomatic complexity, commit frequency, and review cycle time. The results demonstrate that the Informer model outperforms others, achieving an <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$R^{2}$</tex> of 0.9250 and an MAE of 3.60, particularly excelling in long-term forecasting. This model's ability to predict TD with high accuracy makes it suitable for integration into CI/CD pipelines, ensuring proactive TD management. The proposed framework provides actionable insights for developers, enabling them to prioritize remediation based on the most significant factors contributing to TD. This research offers a scalable, data-driven solution for software maintenance, contributing to improved software quality and sustainability.
title_zh: 使用机器学习估算技术债务：利用代码复杂性与开发者交互
abstract_zh: 估算技术债务（TD）对于管理大规模软件项目至关重要。本研究提出了一种基于机器学习的开源Java项目技术债务估算框架，该框架整合了代码复杂度指标与开发者交互特征。利用从10万个GitHub仓库中提取的5500万个Java文件构成的数据集，我们应用了XGBoost、LSTM和Informer模型进行多时间跨度的技术债务预测。采用SHAP（沙普利可加性解释）方法增强模型可解释性，揭示了循环复杂度、提交频率和审查周期等关键技术债务驱动因素。结果表明，Informer模型表现最优，其决定系数（R²）达到0.9250，平均绝对误差（MAE）为3.60，尤其在长期预测中优势显著。该模型能够高精度预测技术债务，适合集成到CI/CD流水线中，从而实现主动的技术债务管理。所提出的框架为开发者提供了可操作的见解，使其能够根据贡献技术债务的最主要因素优先进行修复。本研究为软件维护提供了一种可扩展的数据驱动解决方案，有助于提升软件质量与可持续性。

title: Retrieving Delta Permittivity Using Highly Independent Dual-Band Microwave Sensor with Alternately Reference Capabilities
authors: Syah Alam, Indra Surjati, Lydia Sari, R. Deiny Mardian, Fitri Kurnia Sari, Teguh Firmansyah, Muhammad Iqbal, Nugraha, Dian Widi Astuti, Mudrik Alaydrus, Yuyu Wahyu, Zahriladha Zakaria
abstract: This paper proposes a delta permittivity detection using a microwave sensor based on dual-opposite interdigitated split ring resonator with alternately reference capabilities. The proposed sensor has two different sensing areas and has high independent characteristics operating at dual-band resonance frequencies at fr1 = 0.94 GHz and fr2 = 1.59 GHz. The delta permittivity is detected by placing the reference sample and the measured sample in each sensing area alternately and simultaneously in direct contact. The permittivity of the reference material has a fixed value using vacuum (εr = 1) and RO5880 (εr = 2.2), while the permittivity of the measured sample varies in the range 1 - 9.2. From the measurement results, the sensor has a high performance with a maximum ΔF of 0.81 GHz and 0.84 GHz, Frequency Detection Resolution (FDR) of 0.45 GHz and 0.48 GHz and Normalized Sensitivity (NS) of 6.00% and 6.03% for vacuum and RO5880 as reference, respectively. In addition, the average accuracy of the sensor is in the range of 91.46% - 97.40% for the detection of delta permittivity of the sample. Both sensing areas can be used as references interchangeably and have high performance, respectively. Therefore, the proposed sensor can be recommended for several applications such as material quality control, pharmaceutical industry and food quality control.
title_zh: 使用具有交替参考能力的高独立性双频微波传感器提取Δ介电常数
abstract_zh: 本文提出了一种基于双对交错开口环谐振器的微波传感器，用于介电常数差异检测，该传感器具有交替参考功能。所提出的传感器包含两个不同的传感区域，并在双频谐振频率（fr1 = 0.94 GHz 和 fr2 = 1.59 GHz）下具有高度独立的特性。通过将参考样品和待测样品交替且同时直接接触地放置在每个传感区域中，来检测介电常数差异。参考材料的介电常数为固定值，使用真空（εr = 1）和 RO5880（εr = 2.2），而待测样品的介电常数在1至9.2范围内变化。测量结果表明，该传感器性能优异，以真空和RO5880为参考时，最大ΔF分别为0.81 GHz和0.84 GHz，频率检测分辨率（FDR）分别为0.45 GHz和0.48 GHz，归一化灵敏度（NS）分别为6.00%和6.03%。此外，在检测样品介电常数差异时，传感器的平均准确率在91.46%至97.40%之间。两个传感区域均可互换地作为参考区域，并各自具有高性能。因此，所提出的传感器可推荐用于多种应用，如材料质量控制、制药行业和食品质量控制。

title: Researching the Efficiency of Source Code Defect Prediction Using a Modified XGBoost Algorithm
authors: Timur Fatkhulin, Yuri Leokhin, Svetlana Dymkova
publish: 2026 Wave Electronics and its Application in Information and Telecommunication Systems (WECONF)
abstract: This paper presents an analysis of modifications to the XGBoost machine learning algorithm. The aim of the study is to improve the efficiency of source code defect prediction using a modified version of the XGBoost algorithm. The relevance of the study is due to the need for timely defect prediction in source code, which allows both enterprise equipment and various critical infrastructure to function in the required mode. It is important to note that in the conditions of the modern information society, the correct functioning of computing systems and network equipment is an integral component. Taking this into account, predicting defects in program code is extremely important. During the study, experiments were conducted to train XGBoost models using the modifications considered. The experimental results were analyzed, and a conclusion was made on the most suitable method of modifying the algorithm for solving the problem of predicting defects in source code. The object of the study is defects in program code. The subject of the study are metrics for assessing the quality of error prediction in source code.
title_zh: 基于改进XGBoost算法的源代码缺陷预测效率研究
abstract_zh: 本文分析了XGBoost机器学习算法的改进方案。研究旨在通过改进版XGBoost算法提升源代码缺陷预测的效率。该研究的现实意义在于，对源代码缺陷进行及时预判，能够保障企业设备及各类关键基础设施维持正常运行状态。需要指出的是，在现代信息社会背景下，计算系统与网络设备的稳定运行已成为不可或缺的组成部分。基于此，程序代码的缺陷预测具有极其重要的意义。研究过程中，基于所探讨的改进方案开展了XGBoost模型训练实验，并对实验结果进行了分析，最终得出了最适合解决源代码缺陷预测问题的算法改进方案。研究对象为程序代码中的缺陷，研究内容为源代码错误预测质量的评估指标。

title: Enhancing Code Generation Using Instruction-Tuned Transformer Models for Multi-Language Software Development
authors: A. Sathiyavathi, C. Gethara Gowri, R. Sundar, J. Arockia Theo, L. Poornimadevi, Suberiya Begum S
publish: 2026 IEEE 2nd International Conference on Quantum Photonics, Artificial Intelligence &amp; Networking (QPAIN)
abstract: The rapid growth of multi-language software development has increased the demand for intelligent systems capable of generating accurate and reliable source code from natural language instructions. Transformer-based models have shown significant potential in automated code generation; however, their effectiveness is often limited by inadequate alignment with user intent and challenges in cross-language generalization. This research investigates the enhancement of code generation using instruction-tuned transformer models to improve adaptability across multiple programming languages. A structured experimental methodology is adopted, incorporating a pretrained transformer baseline, instruction tuning, and systematic evaluation supported by a built-in benchmark dataset. Exploratory data analysis and visualization techniques are employed to understand data characteristics and support reliable interpretation of results. The findings demonstrate that instruction tuning improves instruction adherence, code consistency, and generalization capability in multi-language scenarios. The proposed framework provides a reproducible and scalable foundation for advancing automated code generation systems in modern software development environments.
title_zh: 使用指令调优的Transformer模型增强多语言软件开发中的代码生成
abstract_zh: 多语言软件开发的快速增长增加了对能够从自然语言指令生成准确可靠源代码的智能系统的需求。基于Transformer的模型在自动代码生成方面展现出显著潜力；然而，其有效性常因与用户意图对齐不足以及跨语言泛化挑战而受限。本研究探讨了如何通过指令调优的Transformer模型提升代码生成能力，以增强跨多种编程语言的适应性。采用结构化实验方法，包括预训练Transformer基线、指令调优以及基于内置基准数据集进行系统评估。运用探索性数据分析和可视化技术以理解数据特征并支持对结果的可靠解释。研究结果表明，指令调优改善了多语言场景下的指令遵循、代码一致性和泛化能力。所提出的框架为在现代软件开发环境中推进自动代码生成系统提供了可重复且可扩展的基础。

title: Federated and Privacy-Preserving Cross-Project Defect Prediction with Heterogeneous Features_supp1-3692541.pdf
authors: Meetesh Nevendra
abstract: Organizations are often reluctant to share defectlabeled source code because of privacy and intellectual property concerns, yet effective cross-project defect prediction (CPDP) depends on learning from diverse projects. This paper introduces FedCPDP, a federated and privacy-preserving framework that enables collaborative defect prediction without exchanging raw project data and without requiring a common feature schema. The approach employs feature hashing to unify heterogeneous metric spaces, integrates federated proximal optimization with representation alignment to mitigate distribution shift, and incorporates differential privacy mechanisms to safeguard sensitive updates. To further enhance robustness, FedCPDP explores adaptive server aggregation strategies (attention and graphbased) and seed ensembling, while addressing class imbalance through focal loss and threshold optimization. The framework is evaluated on thirty benchmark datasets across five CPDP families under leave-one-project-out validation, using both threshold-free and effort-aware metrics. Results show that FedCPDP improves F1 by up to 10-15% over FedAvg and 6-8% over FedDPI, while achieving 8-10% higher Popt under effort-aware evaluation. These gains are statistically significant, confirming that FedCPDP consistently outperforms state-of-the-art methods while uniquely supporting heterogeneous features and offering explicit privacy guarantees. These findings establish FedCPDP as a practical step toward enabling secure and deployable defect prediction in multiorganizational settings.
title_zh: 基于联邦学习与隐私保护的异构特征跨项目缺陷预测_补充材料1-3692541.pdf
abstract_zh: 组织机构常因隐私和知识产权问题而不愿共享带缺陷标签的源代码，然而有效的跨项目缺陷预测（CPDP）依赖于从不同项目中进行学习。本文提出FedCPDP，一个联邦化且保护隐私的框架，能够在无需交换原始项目数据且无需统一特征模式的情况下实现协作式缺陷预测。该方法采用特征哈希统一异构度量空间，通过联邦近端优化与表示对齐相结合来缓解分布偏移，并引入差分隐私机制以保护敏感更新。为进一步增强鲁棒性，FedCPDP探索了自适应服务器聚合策略（基于注意力与图结构）及种子集成，同时通过焦点损失和阈值优化解决类别不平衡问题。该框架在五个CPDP类别的三十个基准数据集上采用留一项目验证法进行评估，使用了无阈值和考虑工作量的指标。结果表明，相比FedAvg和FedDPI，FedCPDP分别将F1值提升10-15%和6-8%，并在工作量感知评估中实现Popt指标提高8-10%。这些提升具有统计显著性，证实FedCPDP在持续优于现有技术的同时，独特地支持异构特征并提供了明确的隐私保障。这些发现表明FedCPDP是迈向在多组织环境中实现安全可部署缺陷预测的实用一步。

title: Minimization of Reversible Logic Circuits Using A* with Lookahead
authors: Hyunsu Choi, Takashi Hirayama, Katsuhisa Yamanaka
publish: 2026 IEEE 56th International Symposium on Multiple-Valued Logic (ISMVL)
abstract: This paper presents an approach for minimizing reversible logic circuits composed of Toffoli gates, employing the A* with Lookahead (AL*) algorithm. Previous A*-based optimization methods have suffered from excessive memory requirements, which limit the size of circuits that can be handled. To overcome this issue, we employ AL*, an algorithm that incorporates the advantages of depth-first search (DFS) into A*. By predicting the expansion priority of each node through a DFS-based lookahead, the proposed method achieves a significant reduction in memory usage. Consequently, our approach optimizes larger circuits that previous A* methods could not handle due to memory overflow, and we present comparative results in this paper.
title_zh: 使用带前瞻的A*算法最小化可逆逻辑电路
abstract_zh: 本文提出了一种基于前瞻A*算法（AL*）的托佛利门可逆逻辑电路最小化方法。此前基于A*的优化方法受制于过高的内存需求，导致可处理的电路规模受限。为解决该问题，我们采用AL*算法，该算法将深度优先搜索（DFS）的优势融入A*框架。通过基于DFS的前瞻预测来推定节点扩展优先级，所提方法显著降低了内存使用量。因此，本方法能够优化此前A*方法因内存溢出无法处理的大型电路，并在文中给出了对比结果。

title: An LLM-Based Agent Framework for Intelligent Power Module Design
authors: Ruiting Ke, Jianfeng Tao, Xiaojian Ding, Chengliang Liu
publish: 2026 12th International Conference on Electrical Engineering, Control and Robotics (EECR)
abstract: This paper proposes a large language model (LLM)driven agent for power module design. Unlike conventional automation pipelines, it employs a standardized tool interface contract (description-schema-function) to encapsulate modeling, simulation, optimization, and validity checking into a callable tool pool. This enables the LLM to select and orchestrate tools at runtime based on intent captured from dialogue and intermediate feedback. Most tools in this pool are specialized for power module design tasks, such as topology is encoded with a directed adjacency matrix for geometric modeling, and check tools flag die overlaps and boundary violations. For robust execution, constraints are handled in two layers: (i) hard constraints are enforced within tool functions or immutable deterministic chains; (ii) instance-dependent constraints are evaluated by validity checking tools or via log parsing, with results fed back to the LLM. An agent-in-the-loop chip placement optimization task validates the agent's effectiveness, and the implementation is released as open source at https://github.com/henkuailederen/Agent-power-module-design.
title_zh: 基于LLM的智能功率模块设计智能体框架
abstract_zh: 本文提出了一种基于大型语言模型（LLM）驱动的智能体，用于功率模块设计。与传统自动化流水线不同，该方法采用标准化的工具接口契约（描述-模式-函数），将建模、仿真、优化和有效性检查封装为可调用的工具池。这使得LLM能够根据对话和中间反馈中捕获的意图，在运行时选择并编排工具。该工具池中的大多数工具专门针对功率模块设计任务，例如拓扑结构通过有向邻接矩阵编码用于几何建模，检查工具可标记芯片重叠和边界违规。为确保稳健执行，约束条件通过两层机制处理：（i）硬约束在工具函数或不可变的确定性链中强制执行；（ii）实例依赖的约束通过有效性检查工具或日志解析进行评估，并将结果反馈给LLM。通过智能体参与芯片布局优化任务验证了该方法的有效性，相关实现已在https://github.com/henkuailederen/Agent-power-module-design 开源发布。

title: SONIC: Event-Driven Gate-Level Simulator of Ternary VLSI Circuits using Delta Cycles
authors: Steven Bos, Sondre Bitubekk, Ole Christian Moholth, Henning Gundersen
publish: 2026 IEEE 56th International Symposium on Multiple-Valued Logic (ISMVL)
abstract: The simulation of ternary and mixed-radix very large scale integration (VLSI) circuits has largely been unaddressed in multiple-valued logic (MVL) literature. In this work we present SONIC; a zero-delay, event-driven gate-level simulator that iterates delta cycles to quiescence per stimulus. This novel and open source MVL electronic design automation (EDA) tool enables for the first time simulation of ternary integrated circuits (SONIC) with VLSI designs (eg. 10.000+ switching devices). It can automate post-synthesis verification of our REBEL-2 ternary CPU and comes with a REBEL-2 assembler to program it. We report that SONIC drastically improves the simulation speed of our Mixed Radix Circuit Synthesizer (MRCS) by 5-6 orders of magnitude (120.000x - 1.6Mx) on various benchmark circuits. The C#/.Net8 simulator features design rule checks (DRC), a benchmark suite, binary coded ternary (BCT) Verilog export to test on FPGA’ss or tape-out as ASIC, a database and a command line interface (CLI) for integration in other EDA tooling.
title_zh: SONIC：使用Delta循环的三值VLSI电路事件驱动门级模拟器
abstract_zh: 在三值及混合进制超大规模集成电路（VLSI）的仿真方面，多值逻辑（MVL）文献中鲜有涉及。本文提出SONIC：一种零延迟、事件驱动的门级仿真器，通过迭代delta周期直至每个激励达到稳态。这一新颖的开源MVL电子设计自动化（EDA）工具首次实现了对包含VLSI设计（例如超过1万个开关器件）的三值集成电路的仿真。它可自动化完成REBEL-2三值CPU的综合后验证，并附带REBEL-2汇编器用于编程。我们报告称，在各种基准电路上，SONIC将混合进制电路合成器（MRCS）的仿真速度提升了5至6个数量级（12万倍至160万倍）。该C#/.Net8仿真器具备设计规则检查（DRC）、基准测试套件、可输出用于FPGA验证或流片为ASIC的二进制编码三进制（BCT）Verilog、数据库以及用于集成到其他EDA工具的命令行界面（CLI）。

title: Learn to Generate Complementary March Tests
authors: Ruiqi Zhu, Houjun Wang, Chongtao Guo, Yindong Xiao, Zhijian Dai, Rui Wu, Wanyu Yang, Geoffrey Ye Li
publish: IEEE Transactions on Very Large Scale Integration (VLSI) Systems
abstract: March tests require balancing fault coverage (FC) and test length in embedded memory testing. This brief proposes a novel reinforcement-learning (RL)-based framework that learns optimal March tests by exploiting undetected fault statistics. First, we analyze March tests using a scalable state-tuple-based fault analyzer. Then, we formulate March test generation as a problem to maximize expected FC (EFC) under budget limits. By modeling this as a Markov decision process (MDP), we leverage deep Q-learning to find the optimal operation sequences in March tests, with rewards derived from the proposed fault analyzer. Experimental results demonstrate that our framework generates complementary March tests achieving complete residual FC across a broader range of fault models than the existing methods, with generation efficiency significantly improved through fault analysis completing in milliseconds.
title_zh: 学习生成互补March测试
abstract_zh: 在嵌入式存储器测试中，March测试需要在故障覆盖率（FC）与测试长度之间取得平衡。本文提出了一种基于强化学习（RL）的新型框架，通过利用未检测故障的统计信息来学习最优的March测试。首先，我们采用可扩展的基于状态元组的故障分析器对March测试进行分析。随后，我们将March测试生成问题表述为在预算限制下最大化预期故障覆盖率（EFC）的优化问题。通过将其建模为马尔可夫决策过程（MDP），我们利用深度Q学习来寻找March测试中的最优操作序列，其中奖励值来源于所提出的故障分析器。实验结果表明，与现有方法相比，我们的框架生成的互补March测试能够在更广泛的故障模型上实现完整的剩余故障覆盖率，同时毫秒级的故障分析大幅提升了生成效率。

title: Automatic generation method of large model code based on associated AST revision
authors: Yubo Wei, Xiaodong Du, Jianfei Cao, Jiyang Yu
publish: 2025 IEEE 11th International Conference on Control Science and Systems Engineering (ICCSSE)
abstract: With AI’s rapid development, large-model-based automatic code generation has great potential in software development, but existing methods lack functional correctness, code quality and maintainability. This paper proposes a large-model automatic code generation method based on Association Syntax Tree (AST) revision to improve overall performance. It combines NLP and AST structural information to revise large-model-generated code, optimizing grammatical structure, logical clarity and maintainability. Specifically, it uses a pre-trained model to generate initial code from natural language, identifies code problems via semantic association analysis between code and natural language, then revises code with AST structure to ensure correctness and clarity. It further enhances code performance through algorithm selection optimization, automatic code optimization, etc. Experiments on Human Eval, MBPP, APPS, DS-1000 and a custom dataset, using indicators like Pass@k, code complexity and execution efficiency, show this method outperforms existing ones in correctness, quality and maintainability while keeping high efficiency, providing a reliable solution for automatic code generation.
title_zh: 基于关联AST修订的大模型代码自动生成方法
abstract_zh: 随着人工智能的快速发展，基于大模型的自动化代码生成在软件开发中展现出巨大潜力，但现有方法在功能正确性、代码质量和可维护性方面存在不足。本文提出一种基于关联语法树（AST）修订的大模型自动代码生成方法，以提升整体性能。该方法结合自然语言处理（NLP）与AST结构信息，对大型模型生成的代码进行修订，优化其语法结构、逻辑清晰度与可维护性。具体而言，利用预训练模型从自然语言生成初始代码，通过代码与自然语言之间的语义关联分析识别代码问题，再借助AST结构对代码进行修订，确保正确性与清晰度。进一步通过算法选择优化、自动代码优化等手段提升代码性能。在Human Eval、MBPP、APPS、DS-1000及自定义数据集上的实验表明，采用Pass@k、代码复杂度及执行效率等指标，该方法在保持高效率的同时，在正确性、质量及可维护性方面均优于现有方法，为自动代码生成提供了可靠解决方案。

title: Evaluation of the Quality of VLSI Component Placement Using the linear Segment Metric
authors: Vladislav I. Danilchenko, Nikolay D. Posrednikov
publish: 2026 8th International Conference on Information Technologies in Engineering Education (Inforino)
abstract: This study addresses the NP-hard problem of VLSI block placement under technological and physical constraints through the formulation of a multi-objective optimization framework incorporating a comprehensive set of design metrics. In addition to conventional geometric measures—total wirelength, placement density, area utilization, and crossing index—a novel topological metric based on linear segment count is introduced. This metric facilitates not only the minimization of interconnect length but also the structural regularization of routing geometry, thereby improving signal integrity, reducing parasitic effects, and enhancing energy efficiency. To solve the formulated problem, modified bio-inspired heuristics are proposed: a genetic algorithm enhanced with a hybrid evolutionary strategy integrating Lamarckian local adaptation and De Vries stochastic mutations, and an improved stem-cell behavior model incorporating mechanisms for crossing minimization and density-aware placement refinement. A dedicated software framework was developed to conduct simulation-based experiments. The efficacy of the proposed algorithms and the informativeness of the metrics were evaluated using statistical analysis, including correlation studies and Pareto-optimality assessment. The experimental results demonstrate significant improvements in key design parameters such as power dissipation, thermal profile, and routability. Based on the findings, methodological guidelines are provided for integrating advanced topological metrics into contemporary VLSI CAD/EDA design flows, thereby contributing to enhanced design quality, manufacturability, and parametric yield. This work is situated within the domains of physical design automation, computational intelligence in electronic design, and multi-criteria engineering optimization.
title_zh: 使用线性段度量的VLSI元件布局质量评估
abstract_zh: 本研究针对VLSI（超大规模集成电路）在技术和物理约束下的模块布局这一NP难问题，通过构建包含全面设计指标的多目标优化框架进行解决。除传统的几何指标——总互连线长、布局密度、面积利用率和交叉指数外，还引入了一种基于线性段数量的新型拓扑度量。该度量不仅有助于最小化互连长度，还能促进布线几何结构的规则化，从而改善信号完整性、降低寄生效应并提升能效。为解决所提出的问题，本文提出了改进的仿生启发式算法：一种结合拉马克局部适应与德弗里斯随机突变混合进化策略的增强型遗传算法，以及一种集成交叉最小化和密度感知布局优化的改进干细胞行为模型。为开展基于仿真的实验，开发了专用软件框架。通过统计分析（包括相关性研究和帕累托最优性评估），对所提算法的有效性及各指标的信息含量进行了验证。实验结果表明，在功耗、热分布和可布线性等关键设计参数上取得了显著改善。基于研究结果，本文提供了将先进拓扑度量集成到现代VLSI CAD/EDA设计流程中的方法指南，从而有助于提升设计质量、可制造性和参数良率。本研究属于物理设计自动化、电子设计中的计算智能以及多准则工程优化领域。
